title: Proof Techniques, Invariants, and Recurrence Bounds · Counterexample Repair Bay
summary: Review a proposed optimization whose runtime and correctness claims lack evidence. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a formal algorithm review packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.
State initialization, maintenance, and termination for nontrivial loop invariants.
Use contradiction and exchange arguments where direct construction is awkward.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
